# Gnosis VPN Documentation

> Set up and run Gnosis VPN "El Dorado" (beta) — a decentralized VPN built on
> the HOPR mixnet with accounts funded on Gnosis Chain. This release supports
> macOS and Linux (Debian) only; Windows, iOS, Android, and other Linux
> distributions are not supported. Design and commands may change during beta.

## Getting Started
- [Requirements](https://andste.eth.limo/docs/get-started/requirements): System requirements for Gnosis VPN El Dorado — supported platforms (macOS and Debian Linux only; not Windows, iOS, Android, or other Linux distros), minimum hardware (64-bit CPU, 4 GB RAM), and network prerequisites (outbound access and ability to establish VPN tunnels).
- [Installation](https://andste.eth.limo/docs/get-started/installation): How to install Gnosis VPN El Dorado, via either manual installation (download the OS-specific package, then follow the macOS or Debian wiki guide) or the gated onboarding tool for tester-program participants.
- [Funding your account](https://andste.eth.limo/docs/get-started/funding): How to fund your Gnosis VPN account, required before connecting — you need both wxHOPR and xDAI (tokens on Gnosis Chain). Links to the full funding guide, plus faucet funding for users with a secret code.

## Concepts
- [Overview](https://andste.eth.limo/docs/introduction/overview): What Gnosis VPN is — a privacy-focused VPN built on the HOPR mixnet that routes traffic through decentralized infrastructure instead of one centralized provider, and how this differs from a traditional single-provider VPN.
- [The El Dorado Beta](https://andste.eth.limo/docs/introduction/el-dorado-beta): The current beta release (El Dorado, which replaces the earlier Atlantis MVP) — what it supports (functional client, HOPR-based routing, end-to-end encrypted tunnelling, six exit-node locations, on-chain metered payments, macOS and Debian) and what it does not yet support (Windows, non-Debian Linux, mobile).
- [Architecture](https://andste.eth.limo/docs/introduction/architecture): The three main system components — the client (with its root, worker, and CLI binaries), the HOPR network as the decentralized routing layer, and exit nodes (Gnosis VPN server side, running WireGuard) that forward traffic to the public internet.
- [How it works](https://andste.eth.limo/docs/introduction/how-it-works): The connection lifecycle — initiation and local setup, the handshake and WireGuard key registration over the HOPR mixnet, tunnel establishment, and the resulting traffic flow from device through HOPR to the exit node and out to the public internet.

## Troubleshooting
- [Finding your logs](https://andste.eth.limo/docs/troubleshooting/logs): How to get Gnosis VPN client logs — exporting them from the desktop app (Settings → Logs → Export service logs), or finding the log file directly on macOS (/Library/Logs/GnosisVPN/) and Linux (/var/log/gnosisvpn/).
- [Common issues](https://andste.eth.limo/docs/troubleshooting/common-issues): Symptoms and resolution steps for two connection problems — connected but no internet (or unstable) and a connection handshake taking over 60 seconds — with both GUI and CLI (gnosis_vpn-ctl) fixes such as switching exit nodes, checking wallet balance, and restarting the service.

## Reporting
- [Reporting issues](https://andste.eth.limo/docs/reporting/reporting-issues): How to report a Gnosis VPN bug — where to report (GitHub Discussions first, or onboarding-provided support channels), what logs to attach (app export or the macOS/Linux log file), and how to write the report (a [Bug]: title, description, reproduction steps, and environment/version details).

## Optional
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/gnosis/gnosis_vpn): Source code (mostly Shell and TypeScript) and binary build/packaging for the Gnosis VPN client, under the LGPL-3.0 license, with releases, the wiki install guides, and a discussion-first reporting process (bugs start in GitHub Discussions; confirmed ones are promoted to Issues).
- [Onboarding tool](https://self-onboarding.gnosisvpn.com/): Guided early-access onboarding flow for tester-program participants (requires login credentials issued during onboarding).
- [Downloads](https://downloads.vpn.gnosis.eth.limo/): Download page for Gnosis VPN client installers ("Powered by HOPR"). Note: the page is rendered client-side, so automated agents may see an empty shell — the GitHub releases page (github.com/gnosis/gnosis_vpn/releases) lists the actual versioned artifacts.
